labels: Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support

📞 Contact the Professional Legal Team Our team helps with everyday consumer problems—refunds and repairs, misleading prices, subscription traps, unsafe products, scams, energy or telco bills, and rental bond issues. We can explain your options under Australian consumer law, suggest wording for a complaint to a trader, and point you to the right regulator (for example ACCC, energy ombudsman, or tenancy services) when another agency is better placed to act. Before you write: gather receipts or bank lines, contract or plan names, dates you were charged, and any emails or texts with the business—this speeds up triage. Go to Contact us ›

labels: Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Checking a bill line by line Find out how to spot duplicate charges, wrong tariffs and fees you did not agree to.
Utilities and telco complaints Find out internal dispute processes and when an ombudsman or industry scheme can help.
Mistakes and backbilling Find out your rights if a provider seeks large “catch-up” amounts after long errors.
Payment plans and hardship Find out how to ask for affordable arrangements before arrears escalate.
Exit fees and early termination Find out when a fee matches what you were told at sign-up and when it may be unfair.
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Writing an effective complaint Find out what to include in date order so a business can investigate quickly.
Evidence and timelines Find out how to keep emails, photos and logs that strengthen your case.
Internal dispute resolution Find out how to escalate within a company and what deadlines are reasonable.
External dispute schemes Find out when an ombudsman or industry scheme can take a complaint for free.
Going to a tribunal or court Find out when small claims may be suitable and where to get legal information (not legal advice).
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Unfair contract terms Find out which kinds of clauses may be void and how to challenge a one-sided agreement.
Cooling-off and door-to-door contracts Find out when you can cancel within a cooling-off period and how notice must be given.
Subscriptions and auto-renewal Find out how to cancel, what businesses must disclose, and how to dispute “free trial” traps.
Bundled products and add-ons Find out your options when a service was sold with unwanted extras or tied products.
Early termination and exit fees Find out when exit fees are excessive and how to negotiate or complain.
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Cooling-off for unsolicited sales Find out when you can cancel without reason and how long you usually have.
Permits and identification Find out what to ask for before you sign or pay, and how to verify a legitimate trader.
Telemarketing and call lists Find out when calls are restricted and how to opt out or complain about harassment.
Energy and telco marketers Find out common tricks and how to compare offers calmly before switching.
Cooling-off forms and proof Find out how to cancel in writing and keep copies so the business cannot dispute your notice.
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Statutory guarantees and consumer guarantees Find out when the law says a product or service must be of acceptable quality, fit for purpose and match its description.
Major failures and remedies Find out what counts as a major failure and when you can choose a refund, replacement or compensation instead of only a repair.
Repairs, timelines and spare parts Find out how long a reasonable repair should take and what to do if a business delays or refuses a repair.
Unsafe products and recalls Find out how recalls work, what to stop using immediately and how to report an unsafe product.
Digital goods, apps and downloads Find out how guarantees apply to software, subscriptions and digital content you paid for.
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Drip pricing and hidden fees Find out when extra fees must be included upfront and what to do if the final price jumps at checkout.
“Was/now” pricing and discounts Find out what businesses must prove if they advertise a sale price or a limited-time offer.
Bait advertising and stock excuses Find out what to do if a special was advertised but you were pushed to a more expensive item.
Comparisons and fake reviews Find out how misleading endorsements or star ratings may breach consumer law.
Fine print and important terms Find out when important conditions must be prominent, not buried in terms and conditions.
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
When you are entitled to a refund Find out the difference between a change-of-mind policy and a refund you can insist on under the law.
Repair or replace first? Find out when a business can offer a repair instead of a refund, and when you can say no.
Proof of purchase and evidence Find out what receipts, bank statements and photos help if a trader disputes your claim.
Lay-bys and deposits Find out your rights if a business cancels a lay-by or keeps a deposit without a fair reason.
Extended warranties and store policies Find out how optional warranties interact with the free protections you already have under consumer law.
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Online shopping scams Find out how to verify sellers, use secure payment methods and recover funds after fraud.
Phishing and impersonation Find out how to check official contact channels and what to do if your details were stolen.
Unsafe goods and injuries Find out how to report injuries, preserve evidence and alert regulators.
Pyramid and “investment” schemes Find out warning signs and where to report suspected illegal schemes.
Recovery scams Find out why you should be wary of anyone who contacts you “to help” after you have already lost money.
Home — Consumer Rights, information and support Consumer Rights Information & support
Bonds and bond refunds Find out how bonds are lodged, when they should be returned and what deductions must be justified.
Condition reports and photos Find out how to protect yourself at the start and end of a lease with clear evidence.
Urgent and non-urgent repairs Find out who is responsible for repairs and what steps to take if work is delayed.
Rent increases and notices Find out what notice is required and when an increase may be challenged.
Breaking a lease Find out costs, replacement tenants and hardship options if you need to leave early.
